title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: - `Smoke-only pass does not validate normal /vote behavior.` [vote.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md#L17) defines smoke mode as a hard override that skips all real logic, and [check-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh#L178) runs `codex exec "/vote SMOKE_RESULT_MARKER=..."` then only checks three marker lines ([check-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh#L252)). This proves routing/loader, not normal `/vote` semantics. - `No runtime assertion for required bounded-output sections in non-smoke mode.` The prompt requires exact sections (`requested_action`, `why_now`, `affected_scope`, `risk_if_done`, `what_can_continue_without_approval`) in normal mode ([vote.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md#L47)), but verification only checks that these strings exist in prompt text ([check-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh#L209)) and never executes `/vote` against a realistic blocked action to validate output shape. - `No negative runtime test that /vote refuses misuse categories.` Prompt specifies `/vote` must return `BLOCKED` for misuse (outside approval categories, broad actions, state redefinition) ([vote.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md#L58)), but current checks do not run misuse scenarios; `tests/test-codex-vote-prompt.sh` only wraps the check script ([test-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-codex-vote-prompt.sh#L12)). - `Readiness smoke tests mostly validate harness plumbing, not /vote command semantics.` [check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L316) only checks smoke markers, and unit tests use stub scripts like `vote-ok.sh` ([test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L33)). This is good for gate/wiring, insufficient to claim `/vote works normally` end-to-end.
